relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Festus enters the province and quickly goes to Jerusalem.
The leadership renews accusation against Paul.
A request for transfer hides an ambush.
Festus keeps Paul in Caesarea.
Accusers are told to come down and make their case.
The judgment seat is set in Caesarea.
Heavy charges surround Paul but cannot be proven.
Paul denies wrong against Law, temple, and Caesar.
Festus seeks favor by suggesting Jerusalem trial.
Paul claims Caesar’s judgment seat as the lawful place.
He refuses neither death nor justice, and appeals to Caesar.
The appeal sends him toward Caesar.
Agrippa and Bernice arrive at Caesarea.
Festus explains the prisoner inherited from Felix.
Jerusalem leaders had asked for sentence.
Roman custom requires face-to-face accusation and defense.
Festus acted quickly once accusers arrived.
The charges were not the crimes he expected.
The dispute centers on religion and a dead Jesus whom Paul says lives.
Festus is perplexed by the inquiry.
Paul’s appeal holds him for the Emperor.
Agrippa wishes to hear him.
Display, authority, and ceremony gather around the prisoner.
Festus frames Paul as one demanded dead by the crowd.
Yet Festus finds nothing death-worthy.
Agrippa’s hearing is needed so Festus can write something coherent.
Sending a prisoner without charges appears unreasonable.
